cpu: AMD A10-7800 Radeon R7, 12 Compute Cores 4C+8G
notes:
Day 0 - (After Sept. 13 game patch Pre-release)

Proton 3.7-6 Beta - [UN-CHECK] "Use this tool ...". No launch options. 

After pressing PLAY, a warning will appear for no display adapter installed. Just click 'OK'.

TAA, BTAO, Motion Blur, Screen Space Reflections all cause blinking spots (speckles, freckles) artifacts on Lara's skin. Turn off, then other settings can be fine-tuned.

Tessellation causes black patches on some surfaces. Turn off.

No controller rumble in Linux - - - as usual. 'Run Benchmark'/(start button) does not work.

POTATO REPORTS:
LOW Potato - A10-7800/GTX 660SC. Mint 18.3 (4.15.0-34, Nvidia 396.51). 1280x720. Lowest all. Exclusive Full Screen - ON. CPU 'performance' toggled. Compsitor effects off. Benchmark Average FPS: 24.
HIGH Potato - i5-3750 (4.0 GHz set mobo)/1070 Ti SC BLACK. SteamOS beta 2.166 (Linux 4.16, Nvidia 396.54). 2560x1440. Ultra all (except noted settings w/ bugs). Exclusive Full Screen - ON. Vsync - OFF. SMAA. CPU 'performance' toggled. Steamos/BPM session. Benchmark Average FPS: 40. (Same settings @ 1920x1080 - Benchmark Average FPS: 50.)

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Requires upgrading to dxvk 0.72 to fix some graphical artifacts.

4k has noticeable issues with scene transitions and isn't really playable.  Plays much smoother at 1080p (60fps generally with the occasional hiccup).  There is also a beta build for the game that seems to have some performance improvements.

Complains about lack of display adapter and "Tomb raider servers are unavailable" so networked functionality may not work.

Resolution: 1920x1080
Anti-Aliasing: SMAA (other modes cause artifacts)
Texture Quality: Ultra
Texture Filtering: 16x Anisotropic
Shadow Quality: Ultra
Ambient Occlusion: HBAO+ (the other mode had artifacts, off worked too)
Depth of Field: High
Level of Detail: Ultra
Tessellation: On
Bloom: On
Motion Blur: OFF (spots on skin with ON)
Screen Space Reflections: OFF (spots on skin with ON)
Screen Space Contact Shadows: High
Pure Hair: Normal
Volumetric Lighting: On
Lens Flares: On
Screen Effects: On
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Definitely requires upgrading to the latest dxvk - and nvidia beta drivers -  to reach playability at this time. Graphically, the game works well on my system with the following settings: 
Exclusive Fullscreen - ON, 
Resolution - 1920x1080,
Vsync - ON,
AA - SMAA (other settings still cause artifacting),
Texture Quality - High,
Texture Filtering - 16x Anisotropic,
Shadow Quality - High,
Depth of Field - High,
Level of Detail - High,
Ambient Occlusion - HBAO+,
Pure Hair - Normal,
Screen Space Contact Shadows - High,
Motion Blur - OFF (causes issues),
Bloom - ON,
Screen Space Reflections - OFF (causes issues),
Lens Flares - ON,
Screen Effects - ON,
Volumetric Lighting - ON,
Tessellation - ON.

Note: This yields an FPS average of ~40, where running in windows gave me a near-solid 60 in most locations with settings on ultra - most likely due to DX12 (on Windows) and the fact that we are invoking a compatibility layer. Temper your expectations accordingly.

The games is still not faithfully reproduced in it's entirety as there are some bugs here that are not present in Windows - most notably the directionality of sound is not always correct and certain dialog options take upward of 20 seconds to begin once the button is pressed.

All in all, very minor bugs - I expect this game will be Gold, and then Platinum, very soon.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: I have played several hours with the game. Everything is fine except some weird black marks on Lara during the animations. I play the game with vsync on, TAA, HBAO+ and everything set to on. Since my monitor is a 60 fps, I get 60 fps most of the time with some drop in the 50s. The game is fully playable. It doesn't appears to have any cpu bottleneck at all. Another weird issue is that sometime I see some vsync flickering in the upper of the screen even if I vsync is set to on, but it's not appening very often.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: I didn't apply any tweaks, just finished my first playthrough playing the game as is. In this sense, the game runs out of the box, but with some graphical users, but only with those graphical issues. Will apply some of the tweaks mentioned by others in the reports below (above?) with my second playthrough. The game runs good otherwise, no crashes, and you can ignore the "no display adapter" warning, since Windows users report the same problem.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Full playthrough. Runs well in general, but I encountered some notable issues:

1) Sound occasionally drops for a few seconds, but returns quickly.
2) Performance regresses overtime. Restarting the game fixes that. Also using less VRAM-intensive settings delays the effect quite significantly. 
3) Couldn't start the game with native Steam runtime, because it couldn't connect to SE's servers, but that's only required once. So I started the game with the Steam runtime, quit and tried again successfully with the native runtime.

Was able to play mostly at 1080p60 with generally high settings. I suggest, you tinker around with the graphics settings, if you're not happy initially. It pays off!
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes:
Game performance is good and it is playable. You can compare it to native performance on windows. But there are some issues:

1. Without BTAO, Motion Blur, Screen Space Reflections and TAA some graphical settings, skin of any character has random black glitches if it is close (you can notice it mostly on Lara skin). It is still playable with and without those settings and looks fine.

2. There are random bugs with sound. From time to time after sound lags for a while, it will have some high pitched artifacts and reverb. It disappears after some time or after restarting game.

3. Sometimes "black paint spill" effect occurs. Basically some black dark object on screen starts to fill your entire screen and there is nothing to do about it. You won't be able to see anything after that. Getting to main menu and back fixes problem, but some progress occasionally can be lost due to that bug.

4. Game performance is getting down as you play. After several hours you have more lags, more sound glitches, sometimes mouse response is going down. Restarting game resets that. You can slow down this effect by choosing less demand graphics settings.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: This is a Platinum but some video glitches were found. For example black spots blinking around the face of characters (in cut scenes for example), the intro video having lag between the moment the character speaks and when the audio is heard (Audio/Video not in sync), pressing ALT-TAB might make the mouse loose the 360 degree free movement, you would need to press ALT-TAB again or a couple of more times until it fixes it self. There was noticeable lag at some parts in the beginning. But the game DID have every graphic option at max.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: This game is close to being platinum. Currently there are issues with the games implementation of anti-aliasing when it is set to TAA or higher that cause z-fighting issues (flickering black polygons) on character's skin when they are rendered above a certain level (usually Lara, but have seen on the companion character as well). This issue also seems to occur with BTAO enabled. Shadows can be wonky when you enable screen space reflections. Initial launch of the game indicates no VGA detected, but seems to cause no issues otherwise. If we can iron out these graphical glitches (especially in regards to AA) this will be a platinum game.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider
